project: UBC Project STEM Search — Website Design Spec

decorative_elements.science_icons: inline SVG: microscope, magnifying glass, flask, atom, DNA

section_transitions.description: Each section bottom bleeds into next via a large organic SVG blob path that matches next section bg color — no hard horizontal lines

Finding research
shouldn't be daunting.

sections.transition_out: large organic blob wave in #FFFFFF flowing into About section

sections.blob_layout: soft blob mid-right, dot grid top-left

content.label: What is Project STEM Search?
content.headline: Your launchpad into undergraduate research
content.body: As finding research is often a daunting journey, Project STEM Search supports students by bridging the gap between classroom learning and hands-on research. Our supportive interdisciplinary community ensures students build confidence and prepare for a successful career in research and beyond.

cards.title: Unique research experience
cards.body: Personalized guidance to help students land real research positions across all STEM fields.

cards.title: Teamwork through community
cards.body: Hands-on projects and community involvement that build real collaboration skills.

cards.title: Co-op & career ready
cards.body: Resume workshops, interview prep, and cold-email strategies that actually work.

cards.title: Support local organizations
cards.body: Meaningful semester projects that directly benefit Vancouver-area organizations.

sections.transition_out: wave blob in #D0E8F5 flowing into What We Do section

bullets: Network directly with professors and grad students

pillars.title: Resume & Coop Application Workshops

bullets: Learn strategies for contacting professors cold

bullets: Connect with peers across all STEM disciplines

testimonials.quote: I found my summer research position through a professor spotlight event. I had no idea how to even approach a professor before joining PSS — the community completely changed how I think about my academic future.
testimonials.name: Sarah K.

testimonials.quote: The resume workshop completely changed how I approached my RA applications. Got my first position within a month.
testimonials.name: David L.

testimonials.quote: PSS made research feel accessible. I had no idea how to cold-email a professor — now I have a position in a chem lab.
testimonials.name: Priya M.

testimonials.quote: Met my closest study group at a PSS social event. The community genuinely cares about helping each other grow.

sections.transition_out: wave blob in #FFFFFF flowing into FAQ section

sections.blob_layout: medium blob left side, small blob bottom-right

faqs.q: Who can join Project STEM Search?
faqs.a: Any UBC undergraduate student — no prior research experience required. We're built for students at all stages, from first-years just exploring to upper-years ready to apply.
A small AMS club fee applies when you sign up through the AMS portal. The vast majority of our events are free for all members once you've joined.

faqs.q: What STEM fields does PSS cover?
faqs.a: All of them — biology, chemistry, CS, physics, engineering, neuroscience, psychology, statistics, and more. We're interdisciplinary by design.
Roughly 2–3 events per month throughout the academic year (September through April), mixing professor panels, workshops, and socials.

faqs.q: How do I actually find a research position?
faqs.a: Through professor spotlight events, resume/cold-email workshops, and mentorship from upper-year members who've been through the process.

faqs.q: Do I need to commit to attending every event?
faqs.a: Not at all. Come to whatever fits your schedule. We just ask that you RSVP through our Linktree so we can plan capacity.

sections.transition_out: hard cut to navy team section — intentional contrast

sections.blob_layout: very subtle blobs, mostly dark navy

display.type: auto-rotating ticker / infinite horizontal scroll carousel
display.description: Two rows of team cards auto-scrolling in opposite directions (row 1 left→right, row 2 right→left), infinite loop, pause on hover. Each card shows avatar circle with initials + name + role. Card bg: rgba(255,255,255,0.08) with white border.
display.animation: CSS transform translateX infinite loop, ~30s duration, alternating direction per row

content.land_acknowledgement: UBC Project STEM Search operates on the traditional, ancestral, and unceded territory of the xʷməθkʷəy̓əm (Musqueam) people.

global_effects.scroll_reveal: IntersectionObserver, fade-up 28px, staggered delays per card
global_effects.section_transitions: Each section has an absolutely-positioned SVG wave at bottom, colored to match the NEXT section's bg — creates seamless organic flow
global_effects.sparkle_animation: CSS keyframe scale + opacity pulse, 2-4s duration, staggered delays
global_effects.nav_scroll_behavior: Highlight active section link on scroll
global_effects.faq_accordion: Single open at a time, smooth max-height transition
global_effects.team_carousel: Two-row infinite ticker, row-1 scrolls right, row-2 scrolls left, pause on hover via animation-play-state
